title: Cranfillia hirsuta
intro: Cranfillia hirsuta, also known as Red Stemmed Cranfillia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its potential antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cranfillia hirsuta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Cranfillia hirsuta is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cranfillia hirsuta is classified within the family Blechnaceae, which comprises of a group of ferns commonly known as the beech ferns or blechnaceae. This species is a part of the genus Cranfillia, which consists of about 10 species of terrestrial ferns. Cranfillia hirsuta is native to South America, particularly in the tropical regions. It is a type of terrestrial fern that grows in shaded, humid environments. The exact classification of Cranfillia hirsuta within the plant kingdom is further specified as a member of the division Pteridophyta.
